qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated remote code execution via Browser Control API script evaluation endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unauthenticated code execution via the Browser Control API endpoint (/browser-control/session/:sessionId/evaluate). The advisory describes missing authorization on the AI Agent endpoint (POST /apps/:appId/agent). These are different endpoints, different vulnerable components (browser-control module vs agent endpoint), different root causes (missing authentication on a browser automation API vs missing per-app authorization on the agent API), and different vulnerability classes (CWE-306 vs CWE-862).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes missing authorization (CWE-862) on the AI Agent endpoint (POST /apps/:appId/agent) where authenticated users scoped to one app can access other apps' agent endpoints and read-only users receive the full master key. The sole qualifying finding (browser_control_rce) describes unauthenticated script execution via the Browser Control API (/browser-control/session/:sessionId/evaluate), which is a different endpoint, different vulnerable component (browser-control module vs agent endpoint), and different root cause (missing authentication on browser automation API vs missing per-app authorization on agent API). It does not reference the agent endpoint, per-app authorization checks, the readOnlyMasterKey issue, or any code path described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory target: POST /apps/:appId/agent endpoint with missing per-app authorization (CWE-862)
browser_control_rce targets /browser-control/session/:sessionId/evaluate - different endpoint and root cause
No finding references the agent endpoint, per-app authorization, or readOnlyMasterKey restriction
